forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTBN9E9FD9X1PY1ARY9SMN99
+- 01KTBNKCZ2JRRX514XWHPFB7V1
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 8a80
+project: local-review
+title: 'Engine stage 2 ‚Äî fan-out: one specialized review agent per dimension, in parallel'
+---
+## What
+The fleet. Take the `WorkList` and spawn ONE agent per dimension, in parallel, each with a single narrow mandate, the matched rules for that dimension, the dimension's files+diffs, and access to the read-only investigation tools. Each agent returns `Vec<Finding>`. This is the core fix for the user's complaint: duplication/reuse/dead-code/data-driven each get a dedicated agent that cannot get distracted by other layers.
+
+- Render a per-dimension prompt from a template: the dimension's mandate + its rule bodies (verbatim from the matched rules) + the files and semantic diffs + the required output schema (`Vec<Finding>` JSON from the finding-model task). Reuse the validator prompt-rendering approach as the template mechanism, not a new one.
+- Instruct each agent to actively probe with the code_context MCP tools ‚Äî the duplication agent MUST run `find duplicates` + `search symbol` on changed blocks; the dead-code agent MUST run `get callgraph` (inbound) + `get blastradius`; correctness/security read surrounding code. Make these tool affordances explicit per dimension so probing is mandatory, not optional (the behavior the monolithic skill skipped under load).
+- Execute the fleet via the hook-free `execute_agents` primitive extracted in the teardown task ‚Äî parallel with adaptive concurrency, per-call token cap. Backend = the configured `ConnectionTo<Agent>`; default session agent, honor a "force local Llama" flag threaded from the op input.
+- Parse each agent's response with `parse_findings`; tag every finding with its dimension; collect `Vec<Finding>` across the fleet. An agent that errors/times out yields zero findings for its dimension and is logged (via `tracing`), never panics the run.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `run_fleet(work_list, connection, opts) -> Vec<Finding>` exists; spawns one agent per dimension concurrently and aggregates findings.
+- [ ] Per-dimension prompts embed the matched rule bodies and the required `Finding` JSON schema; the duplication/dead-code dimensions explicitly require the corresponding code_context probes.
+- [ ] Backend is configurable (session agent default; local-Llama override honored).
+- [ ] A failing/slow dimension agent degrades to zero findings for that dimension without aborting the others.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Unit/integration test with the mock-agent harness (`PlaybackAgent`/`SessionRecordingAgent`): a 3-dimension `WorkList` ‚Üí 3 agents invoked, scripted responses parsed into the merged `Vec<Finding>` with correct `dimension` tags.
+- [ ] Resilience test: one dimension's agent errors ‚Üí its findings are empty, the rest still return (no deadlock, no panic).
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::fleet` green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî script the mock agents and assert the merged findings first, then implement the renderer + fan-out. Reuse `execute_agents` and the validator prompt-render path; do not reimplement concurrency or templating.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -7,25 +7,25 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: 8a80
 project: local-review
-title: 'Engine stage 2 ‚Äî fan-out: one specialized review agent per dimension, in parallel'
+title: 'Engine stage 2 ‚Äî fan-out: one specialized agent per matched validator, in parallel'
 ---
 ## What
-The fleet. Take the `WorkList` and spawn ONE agent per dimension, in parallel, each with a single narrow mandate, the matched rules for that dimension, the dimension's files+diffs, and access to the read-only investigation tools. Each agent returns `Vec<Finding>`. This is the core fix for the user's complaint: duplication/reuse/dead-code/data-driven each get a dedicated agent that cannot get distracted by other layers.
+The fleet. Take the `WorkList` and spawn ONE agent per matched validator, in parallel ‚Äî the validator is the shard. Each agent gets a single narrow mandate (the validator's `description`), the validator's rules, its declared `probes`, and its files+diffs, and access to the read-only investigation tools. Each agent returns `Vec<Finding>` tagged with the validator name. This is the core fix for the user's complaint: duplication/reuse/dead-code/data-driven are each their OWN focused validator with their own agent that cannot get distracted by other concerns.

-- Render a per-dimension prompt from a template: the dimension's mandate + its rule bodies (verbatim from the matched rules) + the files and semantic diffs + the required output schema (`Vec<Finding>` JSON from the finding-model task). Reuse the validator prompt-rendering approach as the template mechanism, not a new one.
-- Instruct each agent to actively probe with the code_context MCP tools ‚Äî the duplication agent MUST run `find duplicates` + `search symbol` on changed blocks; the dead-code agent MUST run `get callgraph` (inbound) + `get blastradius`; correctness/security read surrounding code. Make these tool affordances explicit per dimension so probing is mandatory, not optional (the behavior the monolithic skill skipped under load).
-- Execute the fleet via the hook-free `execute_agents` primitive extracted in the teardown task ‚Äî parallel with adaptive concurrency, per-call token cap. Backend = the configured `ConnectionTo<Agent>`; default session agent, honor a "force local Llama" flag threaded from the op input.
-- Parse each agent's response with `parse_findings`; tag every finding with its dimension; collect `Vec<Finding>` across the fleet. An agent that errors/times out yields zero findings for its dimension and is logged (via `tracing`), never panics the run.
+- Render a per-validator prompt from a template: the validator's mandate + its rule bodies (verbatim) + the files and diffs + the required output schema (`Vec<Finding>` JSON). Reuse the existing validator prompt-rendering path, not a new one.
+- Make probing mandatory and data-driven: each validator declares `probes` in frontmatter (e.g. `[find_duplicates, search_symbol]` for the duplication validator, `[get_callgraph, get_blastradius]` for dead-code). The prompt instructs the agent it MUST run its declared probes via the code_context MCP tools before reporting ‚Äî this is the behavior the monolithic skill skipped under load.
+- Execute the fleet via the hook-free `execute_agents` primitive (extracted in the teardown task): parallel, adaptive concurrency, per-call token cap. Backend = the configured `ConnectionTo<Agent>`; default session agent, honor a "force local Llama" flag threaded from the op input.
+- Parse each agent's response with `parse_findings`; tag every finding with its validator name; aggregate across the fleet. An agent that errors/times out yields zero findings for its validator and is logged via `tracing`, never panics the run.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `run_fleet(work_list, connection, opts) -> Vec<Finding>` exists; spawns one agent per dimension concurrently and aggregates findings.
-- [ ] Per-dimension prompts embed the matched rule bodies and the required `Finding` JSON schema; the duplication/dead-code dimensions explicitly require the corresponding code_context probes.
+- [ ] `run_fleet(work_list, connection, opts) -> Vec<Finding>` exists; spawns one agent per matched validator concurrently and aggregates findings tagged by validator.
+- [ ] Per-validator prompts embed the validator's rule bodies, its `probes`, and the required `Finding` JSON schema; the agent is instructed to run its probes before reporting.
 - [ ] Backend is configurable (session agent default; local-Llama override honored).
-- [ ] A failing/slow dimension agent degrades to zero findings for that dimension without aborting the others.
+- [ ] A failing/slow validator agent degrades to zero findings for that validator without aborting the others.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Unit/integration test with the mock-agent harness (`PlaybackAgent`/`SessionRecordingAgent`): a 3-dimension `WorkList` ‚Üí 3 agents invoked, scripted responses parsed into the merged `Vec<Finding>` with correct `dimension` tags.
-- [ ] Resilience test: one dimension's agent errors ‚Üí its findings are empty, the rest still return (no deadlock, no panic).
+- [ ] Unit/integration test with the mock-agent harness (`PlaybackAgent`/`SessionRecordingAgent`): a 3-validator `WorkList` ‚Üí 3 agents invoked, scripted responses parsed into the merged `Vec<Finding>` with correct `validator` tags.
+- [ ] Resilience test: one validator's agent errors ‚Üí its findings are empty, the rest still return (no deadlock, no panic).
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::fleet` green.

 ## Workflow

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -10,23 +10,23 @@
 title: 'Engine stage 2 ‚Äî fan-out: one specialized agent per matched validator, in parallel'
 ---
 ## What
-The fleet. Take the `WorkList` and spawn ONE agent per matched validator, in parallel ‚Äî the validator is the shard. Each agent gets a single narrow mandate (the validator's `description`), the validator's rules, its declared `probes`, and its files+diffs, and access to the read-only investigation tools. Each agent returns `Vec<Finding>` tagged with the validator name. This is the core fix for the user's complaint: duplication/reuse/dead-code/data-driven are each their OWN focused validator with their own agent that cannot get distracted by other concerns.
+The fleet. Take the `WorkList` and spawn ONE agent per matched validator, in parallel ‚Äî the validator is the shard. Each agent gets a single narrow mandate (the validator's `description`), the validator's rules, its files+diffs, and ‚Äî crucially ‚Äî the **pre-run probe results injected as ground-truth evidence**. Each agent returns `Vec<Finding>` tagged with the validator name. This is the core fix for the user's complaint: the evidence (duplicates, callers, blast radius) is guaranteed present, so the agent interprets rather than being trusted to fetch it.

-- Render a per-validator prompt from a template: the validator's mandate + its rule bodies (verbatim) + the files and diffs + the required output schema (`Vec<Finding>` JSON). Reuse the existing validator prompt-rendering path, not a new one.
-- Make probing mandatory and data-driven: each validator declares `probes` in frontmatter (e.g. `[find_duplicates, search_symbol]` for the duplication validator, `[get_callgraph, get_blastradius]` for dead-code). The prompt instructs the agent it MUST run its declared probes via the code_context MCP tools before reporting ‚Äî this is the behavior the monolithic skill skipped under load.
-- Execute the fleet via the hook-free `execute_agents` primitive (extracted in the teardown task): parallel, adaptive concurrency, per-call token cap. Backend = the configured `ConnectionTo<Agent>`; default session agent, honor a "force local Llama" flag threaded from the op input.
-- Parse each agent's response with `parse_findings`; tag every finding with its validator name; aggregate across the fleet. An agent that errors/times out yields zero findings for its validator and is logged via `tracing`, never panics the run.
+- Render a per-validator prompt from a template: the mandate + rule bodies (verbatim) + the files/diffs + **the validator's `probe_results` rendered as evidence blocks** (e.g. "Inbound callers of new `fn foo`: none"; "Duplicate candidates for L42: `bar.rs:88` @ 0.94") + the required `Vec<Finding>` JSON schema. Reuse the existing validator prompt-rendering path, not a new one.
+- The agent reasons over the injected evidence. It still has the code_context tools available and MAY dig further, but the mandatory probe evidence is already in the prompt ‚Äî it cannot be skipped, because the engine ran it (in the scope stage via the probe registry), not the agent.
+- Execute the fleet via the hook-free `execute_agents` primitive: parallel, adaptive concurrency, per-call token cap. Backend = the configured `ConnectionTo<Agent>`; default session agent, honor a "force local Llama" flag from the op input.
+- Parse each response with `parse_findings`; tag every finding with its validator name; aggregate across the fleet. An agent that errors/times out yields zero findings for its validator and is logged via `tracing`, never panics the run.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `run_fleet(work_list, connection, opts) -> Vec<Finding>` exists; spawns one agent per matched validator concurrently and aggregates findings tagged by validator.
-- [ ] Per-validator prompts embed the validator's rule bodies, its `probes`, and the required `Finding` JSON schema; the agent is instructed to run its probes before reporting.
-- [ ] Backend is configurable (session agent default; local-Llama override honored).
+- [ ] `run_fleet(work_list, connection, opts) -> Vec<Finding>` exists; one agent per matched validator, concurrent, findings tagged by validator.
+- [ ] Each prompt embeds the validator's rule bodies, its `probe_results` as rendered evidence, and the required `Finding` JSON schema ‚Äî the agent does NOT have to call the probe ops itself.
+- [ ] Backend is configurable (session default; local-Llama override honored).
 - [ ] A failing/slow validator agent degrades to zero findings for that validator without aborting the others.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Unit/integration test with the mock-agent harness (`PlaybackAgent`/`SessionRecordingAgent`): a 3-validator `WorkList` ‚Üí 3 agents invoked, scripted responses parsed into the merged `Vec<Finding>` with correct `validator` tags.
-- [ ] Resilience test: one validator's agent errors ‚Üí its findings are empty, the rest still return (no deadlock, no panic).
+- [ ] Mock-agent test (`PlaybackAgent`/`SessionRecordingAgent`): a 3-validator `WorkList` (with probe_results) ‚Üí 3 agents invoked, scripted responses parsed into the merged `Vec<Finding>` with correct `validator` tags; assert the rendered prompt contains the injected probe evidence.
+- [ ] Resilience test: one validator's agent errors ‚Üí its findings empty, the rest still return (no deadlock/panic).
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::fleet` green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî script the mock agents and assert the merged findings first, then implement the renderer + fan-out. Reuse `execute_agents` and the validator prompt-render path; do not reimplement concurrency or templating.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî script the mock agents and assert the merged findings + that probe evidence is in the prompt, then implement the renderer + fan-out. Reuse `execute_agents` and the validator prompt-render path; do not reimplement concurrency or templating.
\ No newline at end of file

This is the core fix for the user's complaint: the evidence (duplicates, callers, blast radius) is guaranteed present, so the agent interprets rather than being trusted to fetch it.
+The fleet. The shard is the validator; the **grain is the file**. Spawn one agent per `(validator, file)` pair, in parallel ‚Äî each agent reviews ONE file against ONE validator's rules, armed with engine-run probe evidence. Each returns `Vec<Finding>` tagged with the validator name. Bounded context per agent = no attention decay; the probes supply the cross-file facts a single file view would otherwise lack.
+
+**Fan-out unit:** `(validator, file)`. To bound agent count on large diffs, BATCH a few files per agent (chunked to fit context comfortably) ‚Äî but never the whole diff. The grain is the file; the batch is just packing. `log()` the batching applied.
+
+**Prompt payload** (the renderer assembles exactly this per agent; reuse the existing validator prompt-render path, do not invent a new template engine):
+1. **Change purpose** (from `WorkList.change_purpose`) ‚Äî why this change exists, so the agent judges intent (e.g. scaffolding vs. dead code).
+2. **Validator instructions** ‚Äî the mandate (`description`), the `rules/*.md` bodies verbatim (incl. their carve-outs), the severity default, and the `Vec<Finding>` output contract (JSON schema + "one concern per item", `file:line`, what/why/suggestion).
+3. **The file under review** ‚Äî `path`; the structured semantic diff (changed entities, before‚Üíafter); the bounded `source_slice` (header + changed entities + window, NOT the whole file); and the `probe_results` rendered as evidence blocks ("Inbound callers of new `fn foo`: none"; "Duplicate candidates for L42: `bar.rs:88` @ 0.94"; "Similar existing code: `util::parse` @ 0.88").
+- Excluded by design: other files' diffs, other validators' rules, whole-codebase dumps. The agent also HAS the code_context tools to dig deeper, but the mandatory facts are pre-injected so a lazy agent can't miss them.

-- Render a per-validator prompt from a template: the mandate + rule bodies (verbatim) + the files/diffs + **the validator's `probe_results` rendered as evidence blocks** (e.g. "Inbound callers of new `fn foo`: none"; "Duplicate candidates for L42: `bar.rs:88` @ 0.94") + the required `Vec<Finding>` JSON schema. Reuse the existing validator prompt-rendering path, not a new one.
-- The agent reasons over the injected evidence. It still has the code_context tools available and MAY dig further, but the mandatory probe evidence is already in the prompt ‚Äî it cannot be skipped, because the engine ran it (in the scope stage via the probe registry), not the agent.
-- Execute the fleet via the hook-free `execute_agents` primitive: parallel, adaptive concurrency, per-call token cap. Backend = the configured `ConnectionTo<Agent>`; default session agent, honor a "force local Llama" flag from the op input.
-- Parse each response with `parse_findings`; tag every finding with its validator name; aggregate across the fleet. An agent that errors/times out yields zero findings for its validator and is logged via `tracing`, never panics the run.
+**Execution:** run via the hook-free `execute_agents` primitive ‚Äî parallel, adaptive concurrency, per-call token cap. Backend = configured `ConnectionTo<Agent>`; default session agent, honor a "force local Llama" flag from the op input. Parse each response with `parse_findings`; tag findings with the validator; aggregate. An agent that errors/times out yields zero findings for that `(validator, file)` and is logged via `tracing`, never panics the run.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `run_fleet(work_list, connection, opts) -> Vec<Finding>` exists; one agent per matched validator, concurrent, findings tagged by validator.
-- [ ] Each prompt embeds the validator's rule bodies, its `probe_results` as rendered evidence, and the required `Finding` JSON schema ‚Äî the agent does NOT have to call the probe ops itself.
-- [ ] Backend is configurable (session default; local-Llama override honored).
-- [ ] A failing/slow validator agent degrades to zero findings for that validator without aborting the others.
+- [ ] `run_fleet(work_list, connection, opts) -> Vec<Finding>` spawns one agent per `(validator, file)` (batched to bound count), concurrent, findings tagged by validator.
+- [ ] The rendered prompt contains exactly the payload above ‚Äî change purpose, validator rules + output contract, and the file's path/diff/`source_slice`/probe evidence ‚Äî and nothing from other files or validators.
+- [ ] Backend configurable (session default; local-Llama override honored); batching is logged.
+- [ ] A failing/slow agent degrades to zero findings for its `(validator, file)` without aborting the others.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Mock-agent test (`PlaybackAgent`/`SessionRecordingAgent`): a 3-validator `WorkList` (with probe_results) ‚Üí 3 agents invoked, scripted responses parsed into the merged `Vec<Finding>` with correct `validator` tags; assert the rendered prompt contains the injected probe evidence.
-- [ ] Resilience test: one validator's agent errors ‚Üí its findings empty, the rest still return (no deadlock/panic).
+- [ ] Mock-agent test (`PlaybackAgent`/`SessionRecordingAgent`): a `WorkList` with 2 validators √ó 2 files ‚Üí 4 agent invocations (or fewer if batched), scripted responses parsed into the merged `Vec<Finding>` with correct `validator` tags; assert the rendered prompt for one pair contains the change purpose + that file's probe evidence and NOT the other file's content.
+- [ ] Batching test: many small files collapse into fewer agents, and the batching is logged.
+- [ ] Resilience test: one `(validator, file)` agent errors ‚Üí its findings empty, the rest still return (no deadlock/panic).
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::fleet` green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî script the mock agents and assert the merged findings + that probe evidence is in the prompt, then implement the renderer + fan-out. Reuse `execute_agents` and the validator prompt-render path; do not reimplement concurrency or templating.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî script the mock agents and assert the merged findings + the exact prompt payload first, then implement the renderer + fan-out + batching. Reuse `execute_agents` and the validator prompt-render path; do not reimplement concurrency or templating.
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -10,31 +10,31 @@

 ---

+The fleet. The shard is the validator; the **grain is the file**. Produce one agent task per `(validator, file)` pair and submit them to the shared `AgentPool` ‚Äî each task reviews ONE file against ONE validator's rules, armed with engine-run probe evidence, and returns `Vec<Finding>` tagged with the validator name. Bounded context per task = no attention decay; the probes supply the cross-file facts a single file view would otherwise lack.

-**Fan-out unit:** `(validator, file)`. To bound agent count on large diffs, BATCH a few files per agent (chunked to fit context comfortably) ‚Äî but never the whole diff. The grain is the file; the batch is just packing. `log()` the batching applied.

Fan-out submits all `(validator, file)` tasks; the pool queues and drains them. As each task returns, its findings flow to the verify stage's inline guard, which may enqueue verify tasks onto the **same** pool ‚Äî so fan-out and verify pipeline together (no stage barrier until synthesis).

-**Prompt payload** (the renderer assembles exactly this per agent; reuse the existing validator prompt-render path, do not invent a new template engine):
+**Prompt payload** (the renderer assembles exactly this per task; reuse the existing validator prompt-render path, do not invent a new template engine):
 1. **Change purpose** (from `WorkList.change_purpose`) ‚Äî why this change exists, so the agent judges intent (e.g. scaffolding vs. dead code).
-2. **Validator instructions** ‚Äî the mandate (`description`), the `rules/*.md` bodies verbatim (incl. their carve-outs), the severity default, and the output contract. The output contract requires each finding to emit: `rule` (which rule of this validator fired), `claim` (what's wrong AND why it matters), `evidence` (the proof it relied on ‚Äî cite the injected probe result, e.g. "per `duplicates`: 0.94 at `bar.rs:88`"), `suggestion` (the fix). One concern per finding; `file:line` citation. This matches the `Finding` type exactly.
+2. **Validator instructions** ‚Äî the mandate (`description`), the `rules/*.md` bodies verbatim (incl. carve-outs), the severity default, and the output contract. The output contract requires each finding to emit: `rule` (which rule of this validator fired), `claim` (what's wrong AND why it matters), `evidence` (the proof ‚Äî cite the injected probe result, e.g. "per `duplicates`: 0.94 at `bar.rs:88`"), `suggestion` (the fix). One concern per finding; `file:line` citation. Matches the `Finding` type exactly.
 3. **The file under review** ‚Äî `path`; the structured semantic diff (changed entities, before‚Üíafter); the bounded `source_slice` (header + changed entities + window, NOT the whole file); and the `probe_results` rendered as evidence blocks ("Inbound callers of new `fn foo`: none"; "Duplicate candidates for L42: `bar.rs:88` @ 0.94"; "Similar existing code: `util::parse` @ 0.88").
 - Excluded by design: other files' diffs, other validators' rules, whole-codebase dumps. The agent also HAS the code_context tools to dig deeper, but the mandatory facts are pre-injected so a lazy agent can't miss them.

-**Execution:** submit via `execute_agents` (backend = configured `ConnectionTo<Agent>`; default session agent, honor "force local Llama" from the op). Parse each response with `parse_findings`; tag findings with the validator; aggregate. An agent that errors/times out yields zero findings for that `(validator, file)` and is logged via `tracing`, never panics the run.
+**Result handling:** parse each returned task with `parse_findings`; tag findings with the validator; hand them to the verify guard. A task that errors/times out yields zero findings for that `(validator, file)` and is logged via `tracing`, never panics the run.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `run_fleet(work_list, connection, opts) -> Vec<Finding>` spawns one agent per `(validator, file)` (batched to bound count) via `execute_agents`, findings tagged by validator.
-- [ ] The rendered prompt contains exactly the payload above; the output contract instructs the agent to emit `rule` + `claim`(what+why) + `evidence`(cited probe proof) + `suggestion`, matching the `Finding` type.
-- [ ] Concurrency is delegated to `execute_agents` (not re-implemented here); backend pass-through honored; batching is logged.
-- [ ] A failing/slow agent degrades to zero findings for its `(validator, file)` without aborting the others.
+- [ ] Fan-out builds one task per `(validator, file)` (batched) and submits to the shared `AgentPool`; findings are tagged by validator and `rule`.
+- [ ] The rendered prompt contains exactly the payload above; the output contract instructs the agent to emit `rule` + `claim`(what+why) + `evidence`(cited probe proof) + `suggestion`.
+- [ ] No concurrency logic here ‚Äî all execution goes through the pool; backend pass-through honored; batching logged.
+- [ ] A failing/slow task degrades to zero findings for its `(validator, file)` without aborting the others.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Mock-agent test (`PlaybackAgent`/`SessionRecordingAgent`): a `WorkList` with 2 validators √ó 2 files ‚Üí ‚â§4 agent invocations, scripted responses parsed into the merged `Vec<Finding>` with correct `validator`/`rule` tags; assert the rendered prompt for one pair contains the change purpose + that file's probe evidence and NOT the other file's content.
-- [ ] Batching test: many small files collapse into fewer agents, logged.
-- [ ] Resilience test: one `(validator, file)` agent errors ‚Üí its findings empty, the rest still return (no deadlock/panic).
+- [ ] Mock-agent test (`PlaybackAgent`/`SessionRecordingAgent`): a `WorkList` with 2 validators √ó 2 files ‚Üí ‚â§4 tasks submitted, scripted responses parsed into the merged `Vec<Finding>` with correct `validator`/`rule` tags; assert the rendered prompt for one pair contains the change purpose + that file's probe evidence and NOT the other file's content.
+- [ ] Batching test: many small files collapse into fewer tasks, logged.
+- [ ] Resilience test: one task errors ‚Üí its findings empty, the rest still return (no deadlock/panic).
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::fleet` green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî script the mock agents and assert the merged findings + the exact prompt payload (incl. the `rule`/`evidence` contract) first, then implement the renderer + fan-out + batching. Reuse `execute_agents` and the validator prompt-render path; do not reimplement concurrency or templating.
